reasoning: The yfinance tool provides a combined probability estimate of 0.39, driven by a linear model calculation (P=0.46) and a flawed seasonal heuristic. The stock has shown strong recent momentum, rising from ~166 pre-earnings to ~191.7 following a Q1 earnings beat and raised full-year guidance on April 23. This limits the short-term downside trend implied by the linear regression. Because short-term price movements are highly stochastic and the threshold is at a recent local peak, I anchor the 1-week forecast at 0.44 (close to the 0.46 linear model estimate and slightly above the 0.39 combined tool output). Per the forecasting instructions to reflect increasing uncertainty, the estimates steadily revert toward 0.50 for the longer forecast horizons (June, August, and November).
